name: Anguish of the Unredeemed

verdict.issue: Core mechanic, conditions, and scope all match. phase-is:fight + destroyed-by:melee + NOT has-fought-this-phase gating a D6≥2 fight-on-death is exactly what the rule describes.

reasoning: All three gap items resolved: the compound condition gates on fight phase + destroyed-by-melee + not-yet-fought; dice-gated D6≥2 wraps fight-on-death so the 2+ roll is required before the model retains the right to fight.

name: Anchorite Sarcophagus

verdict.issue: Both stat overrides (M set 7, Sv set 3) match the rule values exactly. Scope self/permanent is correct.

reasoning: Sequence pairs the existing M:set:7 with the missing Sv:set:3; both use operation:set per the characteristic-of-N rule for the grammar.

name: Deep Strike

verdict.issue: deep-strike effect type correctly encapsulates Reserves setup and 9" placement restriction. Scope unit/battle is correct.

reasoning: The deep-strike leaf type is the canonical encoding of the full standard rule — Reserves setup, Reinforcements-step placement, and the 9" minimum horizontal distance from all enemy models are invariant for every deep-strike instance, so the grammar deliberately makes the modifier parameterless rather than repeating those constants per instance.

name: Leader

verdict.issue: ability-grant with leader-attachment models the pre-battle CHARACTER→Bodyguard attachment mechanic faithfully. Scope unit/permanent is correct.

reasoning: The Leader rule is a pre-game army-formation action — it governs which CHARACTER units may attach to which Bodyguard units before the battle begins; the grammar has no pre-game phase, no attachment-eligibility condition (CHARACTER keyword pairing with a specific Bodyguard list), and no Attached-unit formation effect type, so the timing and eligibility constraints cannot be expressed; best partial signals the capability via ability-grant.

name: Divine Deliverance

verdict.issue: once-per-battle + start-of-phase + fight phase condition, +3 Attacks on melee weapons, DEVASTATING WOUNDS grant on melee weapons, duration phase — all match the rule precisely.

reasoning: Added start-of-phase and once-per-battle timing conditions to the fight-phase gate; corrected target from unit to self (the rule names this model's weapons specifically); weapon_type:melee narrows both the A+3 modifier and the Devastating Wounds grant to melee weapons only.

name: Rapturous Blows

verdict.issue: The wound-trigger condition is modeled as `was-hit-by-attack` (fires when an attack hits), but the rule says 'each time a melee attack scores a wound' — a wound is a distinct, later step in the attack sequence. An attack can hit but fail the wound roll, so this condition fires in cases the rule does not cover.

reasoning: The wound-scoring step ('scores a wound') sits one position after the hit-roll step in the attack sequence. The grammar contains was-hit-by-attack (anchored at the hit roll) but no was-wounded-by-attack or equivalent; was-hit-by-attack{subject:"target",attack_type:"melee"} is the closest available expression but fires one step early — a hit that fails its wound roll should not trigger the mortal wound. The overall tree structure (activation gate → optional miracle-dice cost → per-attack reactive mortal wound) is correct; only this one condition type cannot be made precise without extending the condition vocabulary.

name: Cherub

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: once-per-battle + after-act-of-faith trigger gates a +1 miracle-dice-pool gain. Modeling once-per-battle as an and-condition alongside the timing trigger is a reasonable DSL choice.

reasoning: The draft used `cp-gain` with a fabricated modifier key; the correct leaf is `resource-gain` targeting the miracle-dice pool, gated on the reactive Act-of-Faith trigger and the once-per-battle frequency, both expressed via `timing-is` conditions per the grammar's frequency-encoding convention — the designer's token note is flavor and is not encoded.

name: Salvationist Medikit

verdict.issue: The rule explicitly states 'if the bearer is on the battlefield' as a precondition; the DSL omits it. Core mechanic (Command phase, D3 resurrection, exclude CHARACTER) is correct.

reasoning: Two gaps remain partially unresolvable: (1) 'bearer is on the battlefield' distinguishes the bearer being deployed from being in Strategic Reserves; no is-deployed or not-in-reserves condition type exists in the grammar, so this condition cannot be expressed and is omitted rather than faked with an unrelated condition. (2) 'excluding CHARACTER models' is added to the resurrection modifier as exclude_keyword, which extends the resurrection modifier schema beyond the canonically listed fields — the canonical list omits resurrection modifier vocabulary entirely, but model-type restrictions on which destroyed models may be returned cannot be expressed as conditional wrappers on the effect without misapplying condition semantics.

name: Simulacrum Imperialis

verdict.issue: The rule grants one D6 roll *per qualifying objective marker* controlled. The DSL models a single-instance trigger rather than a per-objective loop, so it undersells the ability when multiple objectives are held. The per-roll mechanic (4+, gain 1 Miracle dice at face value) is correctly captured.

reasoning: Two structural gaps cannot be fully resolved with the current grammar. (1) 'for each objective marker you control' is an iteration: every qualifying objective produces one independent D6 roll. The DSL has no for-each primitive; the encoding collapses all qualifying objectives into a single dice-gated roll, losing the multiplicative mechanic. This must be tracked as a missing iteration node type. (2) The gained Miracle dice must show a value equal to the roll result, which is mechanically significant (high-value Miracle dice are more flexible). The resource-gain modifier has no value-binding field; value:"roll-result" is added as a schema extension that consumers must interpret. Both gaps require vocabulary additions, not corrected usage.

name: Attached Unit

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (MINISTORUM PRIEST attachment eligibility + conditional Scouts 6" grant) is correct. Two secondary details are imperfect: the Scouts 6" grant condition omits the explicit 'during the Declare Battle Formations step' timing (practically implicit for attachments in 10th ed, but stated in the rule); and target is 'attached-unit' (unit-level) rather than 'that model' (the specific Leader model), which the rule spells out.

reasoning: Encoded as a two-step sequence: the first step grants attachment-eligibility to this unit for MINISTORUM PRIEST leaders (the 'instead of BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD' clause), and the second conditionally grants Scouts 6" to the attached leader model when a MINISTORUM PRIEST is present; the 'Declare Battle Formations' timing is implicit in the Scouts deployment-ability semantic and is not separately encoded.

name: Atttached Unit

verdict.issue: Same structural assessment as 'attached-unit'. The OR-expansion to cover INQUISITOR alongside MINISTORUM PRIEST is correctly modeled. The same two secondary gaps apply: missing Declare Battle Formations timing on the Scouts 6" condition, and attached-unit vs the specific model as the grant target.

reasoning: Identical two-step sequence structure to the BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD variant, but the Scouts 6" condition uses an `or` compound over MINISTORUM PRIEST and INQUISITOR keywords to capture both valid leader types, and the attachment-eligibility grant_type name is updated accordingly.

name: Rituale Nullificatus

verdict.issue: Feel No Pain 4+ against Psychic Attacks OR mortal wounds is correctly captured. The OR operator is the right reading of 'against X and Y' (FNP applies to either type). Threshold, target, scope, and duration are all correct.

reasoning: Wrapped the unscoped FNP 4+ in an `or` compound over `attack-is-type` conditions for `psychic` and `mortal-wounds`, capturing the rule's explicit restriction to those two damage sources; mortal wounds are treated as a pseudo-attack-type within `attack-is-type` since the grammar has no separate mortal-wound damage-source condition.

name: Virtue of Intolerance

verdict.issue: Two secondary imprecisions: (1) The re-roll modifier uses subset 'all-failures', but the rule says 'you can re-roll the Hit roll' — an optional per-attack re-roll of any result, not restricted to failures. Practically equivalent but mechanically different (e.g. interacts differently with abilities that trigger on re-rolls). (2) The embarked exception is modeled as a third sequential ability-grant ('usable-while-embarked') rather than a flag/property on the ability itself, which is a structural mismatch. Core mechanic (quarry designation at battle-start, PRECISION + hit re-roll when targeting quarry) is correctly captured.

reasoning: Quarry selection is modelled as a battle-start ability-grant that assigns a QUARRY marker; target-has-keyword:QUARRY gates the attack bonuses so they apply only to that unit; the TRANSPORT exception is preserved as a third ability-grant because the DSL has no dedicated 'usable-while-embarked' flag — this is the closest canonically-valid leaf.

name: Litany of Deeds

verdict.issue: Core mechanic and all conditions are correctly modeled. Trigger: ADEPTA SORORITAS friendly unit destroyed. Proximity: aura-12 scope correctly credits the 12" requirement. Effect: miracle-dice-reroll-before-pool correctly captures the pre-pool optional re-roll. No phantom conditions; no stated conditions are missing.

reasoning: Reactive trigger: AND-compound of units-destroyed (friendly, current event) and unit-has-keyword:ADEPTA SORORITAS correctly identifies the destruction event; the 12-inch proximity constraint is carried by scope.range:aura-12 rather than duplicated in the condition tree; Miracle dice mechanics are outside the roll-modifier enum so ability-grant with a descriptive grant_type is the canonical encoding.

name: Stanchion of Holy Martyrs

verdict.issue: Correctly models the is-attached condition, Sv set to 2+, and 4+ invulnerable save on the attached unit.

reasoning: The published encoding contained only the stat-modifier setting Sv to 2+; the invulnerable-save step was absent entirely. Both effects are now expressed as sequence steps under the is-attached condition, targeting the attached-unit for the duration the model is leading.

name: Relics of the Matriarchs

verdict.issue: Fully faithful. Timing (start-of-battle-round), selection mechanic (select-up-to-two-relics-of-matriarchs), target (self), and duration (battle-round, covering 'until the start of the next battle round') are all correct.

reasoning: The 'pick up to two from a named pool' mechanic cannot be fully unrolled without the individual relic ability IDs; dice-pool-allocation requires an actual dice pool which is absent here; the draft's invalid max_count field is removed (only grant_type is canonical for ability-grant.modifier); scope.duration:battle-round captures 'until start of next battle round'; the grant_type label communicates both the pool name and the cardinality limit to downstream consumers.

name: Solemn Procession

verdict.issue: Missing the stated condition 'if this model is on the battlefield.' The rule explicitly gates the ability on the model being physically present on the table (ruling out Strategic Reserves, destroyed, etc.). The DSL omits this check entirely. Core mechanic (at start-of-battle-round, set one Miracle dice to value 6 instead of rolling) is otherwise correctly modeled.

reasoning: The 'do not roll; value is 6' mechanic is a Miracle dice value override which has no home in roll-modifier (miracle-dice is not in the roll enum) or stat-modifier (Miracle dice value is not a unit stat); ability-grant with grant_type:miracle-dice-value-set-6 is the canonical label that replaces the non-standard 'fixed-6-override' term from the draft; scope.duration:battle reflects that the ability fires at every battle-round start, not just once.

name: Angelic Ascent

verdict.issue: The rule states the charge ineligibility applies only 'if it does' (i.e. if the unit actually takes the 6" Normal move). The DSL sequences the movement-modifier and ability-grant 'ineligible-to-charge' unconditionally under the same outer condition, meaning the charge restriction would apply even if the player elects not to move. The 'if it does' conditional linking the penalty to the move being taken is absent. Core move mechanic and both effect types are correct.

reasoning: Draft used a single vague movement-modifier and omitted both the engagement-range gate and the charge restriction; the AND-compound of timing-is:after-shooting and NOT engagement-state encodes both prerequisites; the sequence pairs the Normal move (movement-modifier:normal:6) with the consequence (ability-grant:ineligible-to-charge); scope.duration:turn covers the charge restriction lasting until end of turn; behavior:activated reflects that triggering the move is optional.

name: Overseer of Redemption

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: condition (unit contains Repentia Superior), re-roll hit and wound on melee, are all right. Two secondary gaps: (1) subset:'all-failures' is subtly stronger than 'you can re-roll' per attack (rule is per-attack optional, not auto-reroll-all-failures); (2) the effect target is 'unit' but the rule restricts the re-rolls to Sisters Repentia models specifically, not every model in the unit.

reasoning: unit-has-keyword gates both re-rolls on Repentia Superior presence; attack_type:melee narrows to Sisters Repentia attacks (model_filter is forbidden, but Sisters Repentia carry only melee weapons so the narrowing is lossless).

name: Laud Hailer

verdict.issue: Using `ability-grant` with `grant_type: remove-battle-shocked` is the wrong effect type for a status removal — this should be a `remove-status` or equivalent state-change effect, not an ability grant. Trigger (once-per-battle, start-of-phase), target selection (friendly ADEPTA SORORITAS, Battle-shocked, within 12"), and outcome are otherwise correctly represented.

reasoning: The published encoding placed is-battle-shocked in the outer activation gate alongside timing conditions, which evaluates it against the activating model — requiring the source model to be Battle-shocked, which the rule does not state. The fix moves both target-state constraints (ADEPTA SORORITAS keyword and Battle-shocked status) into an inner conditional that wraps the leaf effect, where they are evaluated against the selected target unit. The outer gate now holds only the once-per-battle and start-of-phase timing conditions that govern activation of the ability itself. The 12" range is expressed in scope.range as aura-12.

name: Purge and Cleanse

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: shooting phase trigger, target was hit by ranged attacks (subject:'target' correctly scopes the condition), and the no-Benefit-of-Cover effect for the rest of the phase are all right. Minor modeling issue: the effect specifies target:'enemy-within-aura' while scope.range is 'self', which is contradictory (no aura radius is defined). The rule imposes no distance limit beyond the attack range, so this is a DSL-consistency problem, not a mechanic error.

reasoning: Replaces the draft's erroneous cover-grant with a no-benefit-of-cover denial; was-hit-by-attack {subject:target} is the correct offensive-reactive trigger (enemy unit was hit by this model's ranged attacks); the debuff is applied to that enemy unit until end of phase.

name: Sacred Healing

verdict.issue: Correctly models is-attached + Command phase, the free 1-model resurrection (excluding CHARACTER), and the optional Miracle dice spend path for D3+1 resurrection as a choice construct.

reasoning: The CHARACTER exclusion is added to both resurrection modifiers as exclude_keyword:"CHARACTER". The canonical modifier key list omits resurrection vocabulary entirely; exclude_keyword is the structurally correct position for a model-type restriction and cannot be recast as a condition without misapplying condition semantics (conditions gate whether an effect fires, not which models within a unit are eligible for an effect). The activation structure — choice between a free 1-model return and an optional miracle-dice cost for D3+1 — is unchanged from the draft.

name: Righteous Repugnance

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: shoot/fight trigger, +3 Attacks on both named weapons, miracle-dice gain on kill. The optional nature of the miracle dice spend ('you can discard... if you do') is not modeled — the sequence hardcodes spend → buff with no 'choice' or 'optional' wrapper, making the cost appear mandatory. Contrast with sacred-healing which uses an explicit choice node. The kill-gains-dice part is correct; using OR of melee+ranged covers 'by this model' adequately.

reasoning: Outer sequence combines the activated spend-path (resource-spend gates two weapon_name-narrowed A+3 stat-modifiers) with the reactive enemy-destroyed path (destroyed-by-attack-type or-compound covering both attack types → resource-gain); weapon_name narrowing correctly restricts the Attacks buff to the two named weapons.

name: Cherubs

verdict.issue: Timing (twice-per-battle, after-act-of-faith), effect (gain 1 Miracle dice), and scope (self, permanent) all match the rule exactly.

reasoning: resource-gain encodes the Miracle dice gain mechanic correctly; timing-is conditions capture both the twice-per-battle usage cap and the after-Act-of-Faith reactive trigger; the Designer's Note (physical token tracking) is a UI reminder with no in-battle effect and is not encoded.

name: Storm of Retribution

verdict.issue: The units-destroyed condition uses side:"friendly" but does not filter for the ADEPTA SORORITAS keyword. The rule requires the enemy unit to have destroyed specifically ADEPTA SORORITAS units — any destroyed friendly unit triggers the +1 hit/wound bonus per the DSL. Core mechanic (unconditional re-roll 1s on hit/wound, conditional +1 hit/+1 wound) is structurally correct.

reasoning: Base re-roll ones on hit and wound apply to all ranged attacks; the +1 hit / +1 wound bonus is gated inside a conditional whose condition is units-destroyed(side:friendly, window:battle, count_min:1). This is a global side-level approximation — the grammar has no 'this specific target unit caused the destruction' predicate, so the condition fires whenever any friendly unit has died this battle rather than scoping to which enemy unit did the killing.

name: Impetuous Fervour

verdict.issue: The rule says the full hit re-roll applies 'instead' of the re-roll-1s when near an objective — they are mutually exclusive options. The DSL sequences them additively: step 1 unconditionally fires re-roll-ones, then step 2 conditionally adds re-roll-all-failures. Because re-rolling all failures subsumes re-rolling 1s the net outcome is functionally equivalent, but the 'instead' substitution (mutual exclusivity) is not modeled. Also matters for the once-per-roll rule: if step 1 already consumed the re-roll on a 1, step 2's full re-roll cannot legally fire.

reasoning: Two-tier structure: unconditional re-roll ones on hit fires for every attack; the full re-roll (all-failures) is gated on the target being within range of an objective marker via within-range-of-objective. Because a full re-roll subsumes ones-only, having both in sequence is mechanically correct — the upgrade fires only when the objective condition is satisfied.

name: Sacred Banner

verdict.issue: The rule grants an unconditional option to re-roll Advance and Charge rolls. Using subset:"all-failures" introduces a phantom restriction: for charge rolls, it implies you can only re-roll a failed charge, not a made one; for advance rolls there is no per-roll pass/fail threshold at all, making 'all-failures' semantically undefined. The rule simply says 'you can re-roll' with no subset restriction.

reasoning: The draft omitted the advance re-roll entirely. Both rolls are now encoded as re-roll(all-failures) in a sequence. The roll:"advance" key is not explicitly enumerated in the spec's roll-modifier examples but follows the same pattern as roll:"charge" — it is the natural dice-roll label for advance rolls and is the closest available encoding. Confidence is medium because advance is not listed in the canonical roll-modifier roll set.

name: Lone Operative

verdict.issue: Correctly models the not-attached conditional and the 12" ranged-targeting restriction. Scope self/permanent is appropriate for a passive unit rule.

reasoning: The not(is-attached) wrapper correctly encodes the 'unless part of an Attached unit' exception — when the model is joined to a bodyguard unit, the whole conditional is false and the restriction evaporates. The restriction itself is tightened to ranged-targeting-requires-attacker-within-12, removing the draft's stray 'closest' language which has no basis in the rule text. The rule as written carries no additional visibility condition beyond the base game targeting rules, so none is added.

name: Holy Judgement

verdict.issue: All three steps are correct: mandatory Battle-shock test, -2 to the battle-shock result if CHAOS keyword, 3 mortal wounds on failure. Scope aura-12 covers the 12" targeting range. Visibility is a standard targeting qualifier and its absence in the DSL is not a core-mechanic error. The phase/timing conditional in the effect correctly pins the trigger to start-of-Shooting.

reasoning: The draft collapsed the whole chain to immediate mortal wounds, losing both the battle-shock test and the CHAOS penalty. The repaired sequence fires at start-of-shooting via an and(phase-is, timing-is) gate, then: (1) ability-grant forces the selected enemy unit to take a battle-shock test — the grammar has no 'force-test' primitive so this is the closest expressible leaf; (2) a conditional on target-has-keyword(CHAOS) applies leadership-modifier subtract 2 to the battle-shock test; (3) is-battle-shocked then gates the 3 mortal wounds, correctly triggering only when the forced test is failed. Confidence is medium because ability-grant is an approximation for the test-forcing mechanic.

name: Mysterious Saviours

verdict.issue: The rule states the stratagem costs 0CP — the DSL models this as a 1CP refund. These are not mechanically identical: a refund requires the CP to be available and spent first; a 0CP cost means no CP need be available at all. The limit-exempt grant correctly captures 'even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.'

reasoning: Two distinct effects: cp-refund(stratagem:heroic-intervention, amount:1) makes the 1CP stratagem cost 0CP net; ability-grant(heroic-intervention-stratagem-limit-exempt) encodes the 'even if you have already used that Stratagem this phase' bypass, which is a stratagem-limit meta-rule with no direct grammar primitive. The draft named the wrong stratagem and showed a '?' amount — both are corrected. Confidence is medium because cp-refund modifier keys are not canonically specified and ability-grant is an approximation for the once-per-phase exemption.

name: Self Repair

verdict.issue: Timing (start-of-command-phase), target (bearer), and effect (+1 W) all match the rule. Using duration:permanent for a wound-regain is correct — healed wounds persist rather than expiring.

reasoning: No wound-recovery leaf type exists; stat-modifier W add 1 on the bearer is the closest correct encoding — consumers are expected to cap at the starting W value. resurrection is wrong because it reconstitutes a destroyed model; this model is alive and losing wound tracking is a stat, not a life-state transition.

name: Righteous Paragons

verdict.issue: No issues. OR condition over MONSTER/VEHICLE is correct, +1 hit and +1 wound modifiers are correctly sequenced, scope fits a per-attack conditional on the shooting/fight unit.

reasoning: The draft applied the hit and wound bonuses universally; the fix gates the sequence behind an or-compound target-has-keyword condition (MONSTER or VEHICLE) so the bonuses only apply when attacking those unit types.

name: Rites of Castigation

verdict.issue: The rule restricts the AP bonus to 'friendly ADEPTA SORORITAS unit' attacks. The authored DSL targets 'all-friendly', dropping that keyword restriction entirely. A stated condition is missing: only ADEPTA SORORITAS units should benefit, not every friendly unit.

reasoning: AP subtract 1 correctly improves penetration (e.g. AP 0 → AP -1 in canonical 40k notation). The ADEPTA SORORITAS keyword filter on the benefiting friendly units and the once-per-turn-per-enemy-unit frequency cap are not expressible in the current leaf grammar; both are noted as partial encoding gaps. The was-hit-by-attack subject:target count_min:1 correctly gates on the enemy being hit by this model's own shots.

name: Devastating Refrain

verdict.issue: Both sequence steps capture the rule's mechanics correctly (forced battle-shock on indirect-fire hit; Deadly Demise upgrades to 5+ on destruction). Minor imprecision: 'leadership-modifier' with operation 'force' is a type-name overload — forcing a test is not the same semantics as modifying a characteristic value. Core effect (mandatory battle-shock test) is unambiguously correct from the parameters, so this is a secondary detail, not a wrong mechanic.

reasoning: Part 1: attack_type indirect-fire corrects the draft's weapon_name misuse — Indirect Fire is a weapon ability/keyword category, not a named weapon. Part 2 trigger: destroyed-by-attack-type is the nearest available condition for model destruction, though it operates at unit granularity; the grammar has no model-level destroyed-by condition. Part 2 effect: the grammar has no modify-ability-threshold leaf, so ability-grant deadly-demise-on-5-plus is the least-reimplementing encoding — it expresses that the defender's Deadly Demise fires differently rather than rebuilding the mortal-wounds chain with a phantom enemy-within-aura target.

name: Fiery Conviction

verdict.issue: Option 1 (discard Miracle dice → 1CP) is correct. Option 2's structure (Leadership test → 1CP) is correct, but the dice-gate encodes 2D6 ≥ 7 as passing, whereas 10th-edition Leadership tests pass when 2D6 ≤ the model's Leadership characteristic — the comparison direction is inverted. Core mechanic (Leadership test conditionally yields CP) is preserved; only the pass/fail encoding direction is wrong.

reasoning: Added the choice node for the two distinct options the draft collapsed into a bare cp-gain: option 1 sequences a miracle-dice resource-spend with a cp-gain; option 2 is a dice-gated 2D6 leadership test (threshold 7 as a representative Ld value — actual threshold must reference the model's Ld characteristic, which the DSL cannot resolve dynamically).

name: Holy Mission

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: attachment + unit keyword → correct ability granted for each branch (Scouts 6" / Infiltrators). The 'Declare Battle Formations step' timing is not encoded — the condition checks only is-attached + keyword with no timing constraint. In practice, leader attachment always happens during that step, so this is a missing secondary detail rather than a core error.

reasoning: Replaced the single vague ability-grant with two independent conditioned branches: is-attached AND unit-has-keyword DOMINION SQUAD grants scouts-6; is-attached AND unit-has-keyword SISTERS NOVITIATE SQUAD grants infiltrators. Both use duration:battle since the ability is granted at Declare Battle Formations and persists for the game.

name: War Hymns

verdict.issue: Clean: is-attached condition correctly gates the 'while leading' clause; keyword-grant targets melee weapons on the unit; scope range=unit / duration=battle with the conditional doing the real gating work.

reasoning: The is-attached condition was already correct; adding weapon_type:'melee' to the keyword-grant modifier restricts the buff to melee weapons and fixes the stated gap without any other change.

name: Holy Piety

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct. phase-is:fight is an implicit constraint of 'melee attack' — not a phantom. NOT is-battle-shocked matches the rule's condition. all-failures is the standard DSL representation for a per-attack hit re-roll. Target=bearer is correct.

reasoning: Wrapping the phase-is condition in an AND with a NOT is-battle-shocked operand captures the exclusion of Battle-shocked units; narrowing with attack_type:'melee' and targeting bearer restricts the re-roll to this model's melee attacks only.

name: Embodied Prophecy

verdict.issue: Two-branch sequence covers both cases correctly: NOT charged → choice of one keyword; charged → both granted. duration=phase captures 'until the end of the phase'. No missing or phantom conditions.

reasoning: A top-level sequence with two mutually exclusive conditional steps handles both branches: NOT charged-this-turn fires a player choice between the two keywords, while charged-this-turn fires both as a sequence; weapon_type:'melee' is applied to every keyword-grant in both paths.

name: Righteous Awareness

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is right (D6" normal move when enemy ends move within 9" and not in engagement range). Two gaps: (1) the trigger doesn't restrict to Normal/Advance/Fall Back moves — a Charge move end would also satisfy the authored condition, which the rule excludes; (2) 'once per turn' limit is not explicitly modeled.

reasoning: The reactive trigger (enemy ends a move within 9") is encoded as opponent-unit-within-range with the 9" credited to scope.range; NOT engagement-state captures the engagement-range exclusion; the once-per-turn frequency limit and the specific move-type distinction (Normal/Advance/Fall Back) have no exact DSL equivalents and are omitted.

name: Holy Vanguard

verdict.issue: All three conditions (declare-battle-formations timing, is-attached, embarked-in-transport) match the rule. Effect grants Scouts 6" to the attached-unit (the Leader), target and grant_type are correct, and battle duration is appropriate for a pregame ability.

reasoning: The missing TRANSPORT gate is expressed via engagement-state embarked-in-transport — the closest available condition for being embarked inside a TRANSPORT keyword model at the start of the game. No dedicated is-embarked condition exists; engagement-state with a state parameter is the best available approximation. The timing and is-attached conditions from the draft are retained and correct.

name: Healing Tears

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (command phase, Celestine present, below starting strength → choose 1 or D3 resurrections). The model-type constraint on each option is not captured: option-1 is restricted to a Geminae Superia model and option-2 to Bodyguard models (explicitly excluding Geminae Superia). Both resurrection effects use an untyped target, which would allow returning the wrong model type.

reasoning: Removing the inverted inner conditions from both choice branches yields a clean 'either/or' player choice — restore 1 (Geminae Superia) or D3 (other Bodyguard) — governed only by the correct outer conditions of command-phase, CELESTINE presence, and below-starting-strength.

name: Lifewards

verdict.issue: Condition (unit-has-keyword GEMINAE SUPERIA) faithfully models 'while this unit contains one or more Geminae Superia models'. Effect (FNP 4+ on bearer) and scope (self) correctly represent Celestine gaining Feel No Pain 4+.

reasoning: The draft omitted both the gating condition and the correct target. unit-has-keyword GEMINAE SUPERIA correctly expresses 'while one or more Geminae Superia models remain in the unit' because a unit's keyword set is the union of its constituent models' keywords — once all Geminae are destroyed the keyword is lost and the condition fails. Target bearer isolates the FNP to Celestine rather than granting it to the whole unit.

name: Defenders of the Faith

verdict.issue: Timing (end-of-command-phase), both conditions (within-range-of-objective, controls-objective), and effect (objective-control-modifier sticky:true capturing 'remains under your control until opponent LoC exceeds yours') all match the rule.

reasoning: The draft used the right modifier shape (sticky:true) but stripped the trigger conditions entirely, which is what made it misdescribed. The corrected tree gates the sticky grant on end-of-command-phase timing, the unit being within range of an objective, and that objective being under friendly control — matching the three explicit requirements in the rule. The sticky modifier correctly encodes the 'remains under control until opponent Level of Control exceeds yours' semantic without re-expressing the OC comparison logic.

name: Null Rod

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: FNP 4+ conditioned on mortal wound or psychic attack, scoped to the bearer's unit for the battle. No phantom or missing conditions.

reasoning: Wrapping the feel-no-pain leaf in an or-compound limited to mortal-wound and psychic attack-is-type conditions fixes the over-broad unconditional FNP the draft produced.

name: Auto-Tapestry of the Emperor’s Judgement
reasoning: Adding unit-has-keyword AESTRED-THURGA as a second and-operand alongside is-attached enforces the named-model prerequisite the draft omitted entirely.

name: Recount the Deeds of the Saints

verdict.issue: Step 2's condition (`destroyed-by-attack-type: any`) is missing the stated constraint that the destroyed model must specifically be Agathe Dolan. As written it triggers on any model death in the unit, not only on Agathe Dolan's destruction.

reasoning: First step gates on is-attached + named-model keyword + units-destroyed for the per-kill gain; second step uses destroyed-by-attack-type (attack_type: any) as the closest available proxy for the model-death event, since no general model-destroyed condition exists in the grammar.

name: Emergency Combat Embarkation

verdict.issue: The stated condition that the selected unit must have been chosen as a target of that specific charge is absent. Without it the ability can fire for any eligible Adepta Sororitas unit near the transport, far broader than the rule intends.

reasoning: Charge phase + opponent turn + faction keyword + negated engagement-state + transport-proximity conditions replace the generic stub; the exact 3" numeric range on unit-within-range-of and the sub-phase timing slot (after targets declared, before charge move) have no dedicated grammar parameters so are approximated.

name: Holy Cover

verdict.issue: The rule's second condition — the model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this Fortification — is entirely missing. The DSL grants Benefit of Cover on every ranged attack regardless of line of sight.

reasoning: Replacing the too-broad phase-is condition with per-allocated-attack attack-is-type ranged tightens the trigger correctly; the fortification-specific LOS-blocking predicate has no direct condition-type equivalent, so it remains implicit in the fortification being the ability's bearer.

name: Consecrated Ground
reasoning: Terrain-area-tag anchors the fortification identity; the ADEPTA-SORORITAS + INFANTRY keyword gate covers infantry floor-movement; the unconditioned ADEPTA-SORORITAS check delivers the doubled Acts-of-Faith aura; the terrain-setup placement rule (parts within 1" of each other at deployment) is a pre-game constraint with no in-battle grammar equivalent and is approximated by the terrain-area-tag.

name: Acts of Faith
reasoning: Three-part sequence correctly models: (1) timed resource gain at round start, (2) reactive resource gain on friendly unit destruction, and (3) activated pool-spend that substitutes one dice in any eligible roll. Confidence is medium because resource-gain and resource-spend have no canonical modifier keys in the grammar spec, so the modifier shape (resource, count, value_roll, substitutes, eligible_rolls, max_per_roll) is inferred from the type's purpose rather than a defined contract — the structural tree is accurate but the modifier keys for these two types are not normative.
